article_title: Transparency obligations for certain AI systems
obligation_text: Deployers of an AI system that generates or manipulates image, audio or video content constituting a deep fake, shall disclose that the content has been artificially generated or manipulated. Deployers of an AI system that generates or manipulates text which is published with the purpose of informing the public on matters of public interest shall disclose that the text has been artificially generated or manipulated.
scoring_text: Knowledge of deepfake and AI-generated content disclosure requirements, including identification of content subject to disclosure obligations, disclosure mechanism design, and public interest content transparency requirements.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of deepfake and AI-generated content disclosure requirements, including identification of content subject to disclosure obligations, disclosure mechanism design, and public interest content transparency requirements.
reference_document_element_description: Ability to assess and communicate the security implications of emerging AI technologies, including foundation models, autonomous agents, and AI-generated content, to both technical and executive audiences.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 50(4) (obligation) intersects with WIDAI SP domain. SP-A-003 is the strongest match (STS: 0.422, cosine: 0.541). Secondary: AG-K-013 (AG, cosine: 0.509), AG-K-008 (AG, cosine: 0.508).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.2/10 (raw 0.422). Shared vocabulary: [ai, content, generated]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [deepfake, design, disclosure, identification, interest, knowledge] vs WIDAI terms [ability, agents, assess, audiences, autonomous, communicate] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.874 (P=0.870, R=0.879). Balanced P/R — the 25-word competency implication and 26-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.8%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the EU AI Act element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.541 > STS 0.422 (gap +0.118). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, content, generated] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 20.0%. Shared tokens: [ai, content, generated]. EU AI Act-only: [deepfake, design, disclosure, identification, interest, knowledge]. WIDAI-only: [ability, agents, assess, audiences, autonomous, communicate].
